domain_name: Mobile phones and market efficiency in developing economies

background_concepts.concept: Law of one price / market price dispersion
background_concepts.why_needed: The fishing-market studies argue mobile phones reduce price dispersion across markets, invoking the economic 'law of one price' as the benchmark for market efficiency without explaining it.

suggested_resources.title: The Law of One Price (Corporate Finance Institute)

background_concepts.concept: GSM cellular network architecture (base station, spectrum, backhaul)
background_concepts.why_needed: The community cellular papers assume familiarity with how a GSM cell site works (base transceiver station, spectrum licensing, backhaul to the core network) when describing small-scale locally-run networks.

suggested_resources.title: How Cell Phones Work (U.S. FCC consumer guide)
Cellular Concepts and GSM Basics (NPTEL lecture notes)

domain_name: Rural electrification via microgrids and minigrids

background_concepts.why_needed: These engineering papers design standalone minigrids and DC microgrids assuming the reader knows what a microgrid is, its components (generation, storage, loads), and the concept of islanded vs. grid-connected operation.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrids (DOE Tribal Energy Guide)
Microgrid Conceptual Design Guidebook (Sandia National Laboratories)

background_concepts.why_needed: The electrification-project selection paper is built entirely around choosing an MCDA method (weights, criteria, ranking of alternatives) but does not teach what MCDA is or how weighting/scoring works.

suggested_resources.title: An Introductory Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (UK Government Analysis Function)
A Practical Guide to Multi-Criteria Decision Analysis (Victoria University of Wellington)

domain_name: Electricity provision and the political economy of the grid

background_concepts.why_needed: The election-cycle studies claim causal effects of political incentives on electricity supply using quasi-experimental designs (special elections, difference-in-differences) without explaining the identification strategy or parallel-trends assumption.

suggested_resources.title: Difference-in-Differences (World Bank DIME Wiki)
Difference-in-Differences (Causal Inference: The Mixtape)

background_concepts.concept: Distribution losses / aggregate technical & commercial (AT&C) losses
background_concepts.why_needed: The electricity-loss paper measures 'line losses' as a manipulable political variable, assuming the reader understands technical vs. non-technical (theft/billing) distribution losses in a power grid.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: How electricity is delivered (U.S. EIA)

domain_name: Satellite/remote-sensing mapping of infrastructure and socioeconomic conditions

background_concepts.concept: Semantic segmentation and object detection in imagery
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers infer roads, buildings, and infrastructure from imagery using segmentation and detection architectures, assuming the reader knows pixel-level labeling, bounding boxes, and CNN-based vision pipelines.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)
Semantic Segmentation (MathWorks)

background_concepts.concept: Nighttime lights as a socioeconomic proxy
background_concepts.why_needed: The nighttime-light reconstruction and poverty-mapping papers treat satellite-measured night light as a proxy for economic activity and electrification without explaining the DMSP/VIIRS data or the light-GDP correlation.

suggested_resources.title: Nighttime Lights (NASA Earthdata)

domain_name: AI for power systems modeling and grid resilience

background_concepts.concept: Optimal power flow (OPF) and power flow equations
background_concepts.why_needed: Several papers learn or approximate OPF solutions and use power-flow benchmarks, assuming the reader knows AC/DC power flow equations, generator dispatch, and line-flow constraints.

suggested_resources.title: Introduction to Optimal Power Flow (TU Delft OpenCourseWare)
Lecture Notes on Optimal Power Flow (DTU, Chatzivasileiadis)

background_concepts.why_needed: The fault-chain search and cascading-failure mitigation papers assume understanding of how a single line/component overload propagates into a system-wide blackout.

suggested_resources.title: Tutorial: Cascading Failures (Graph-TIGER documentation)
Grid Systems / Microgrids overview (U.S. DOE Office of Electricity)

domain_name: Solar energy forecasting and rooftop/asset assessment

background_concepts.concept: Photovoltaic energy conversion and system components
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar forecasting and rooftop-potential papers assume familiarity with how PV cells/modules generate power, tilt/orientation, and factors (irradiance, shading, cloud cover) that drive output.

suggested_resources.title: How Does Solar Work? (U.S. DOE)
Photovoltaics and electricity (U.S. EIA Energy Explained)

background_concepts.concept: Oriented object detection in remote sensing
background_concepts.why_needed: The solar-panel mapping paper uses 'oriented' (rotated) bounding boxes rather than standard axis-aligned detection, a specialized variant the reader must understand.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation / object detection (V7 Labs)

domain_name: Sensing and data-driven optimization of local energy demand and services

background_concepts.concept: Electricity load profiles and demand modeling
background_concepts.why_needed: These papers estimate hourly load profiles and induced residential demand, assuming the reader understands load curves, peak/base load, and load factor as characterizations of electricity demand.

suggested_resources.title: Electricity explained: Use of electricity / demand (U.S. EIA)

domain_name: Disaster and climate risk detection for infrastructure resilience

background_concepts.why_needed: The damage-assessment papers classify pre/post-disaster building damage from satellite imagery, assuming knowledge of remote-sensing classification pipelines and change detection.

suggested_resources.title: Beginner's Guide to Semantic Segmentation (V7 Labs)

domain_name: Infrastructure and design in informal settlements
Rural telecenters and shared-access digital service delivery

background_concepts.why_needed: The macro study estimates telecom penetration's effect on GDP growth using cross-country growth regressions and invokes network externalities/critical mass, terms it does not define.

suggested_resources.title: Network Effects / Externalities (Corporate Finance Institute)

background_concepts.concept: Off-grid solar systems: storage sizing and business models
background_concepts.why_needed: The field-pilot paper discusses battery storage, panel sizing, and pay-as-you-go/tariff business models for off-grid microgrids, assuming the reader understands these energy-access fundamentals.

suggested_resources.title: Microgrid Overview (U.S. DOE Grid Deployment Office fact sheet)
Solar Photovoltaic System Design Basics (U.S. DOE)

domain_name: Power grid reliability monitoring and analytics

background_concepts.concept: Energy burden and energy equity metrics
background_concepts.why_needed: The energy-burden and equitable-transition papers center on 'energy burden' (share of household income spent on energy) as a policy metric without defining it.

suggested_resources.title: Low-Income Energy Affordability Data (LEAD) Tool & energy burden (U.S. DOE)

domain_name: Rural and last-mile network connectivity measurement
Digital inclusion for marginalized users and small organizations
